mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 equities and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending power and deteriorating corporate profit margins, which forces S&P 500 prices lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized demand growth across durable and non-durable sectors, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which improves short-term earnings revisions and triggers immediate positive S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of greater than 0.5 percent are followed by negative 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 230.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 230.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS and UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly expands the aggregate earnings base of S&P 500 constituents, creating a persistent upward drift in equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day returns in greater than 50 percent of quarterly observations over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 90.0 index threshold signals severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, which depresses corporate profit forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 90.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of instances.
